summary: Clear audience recording captures an energetic 1984 show highlighted by a smoking China>Rider and a powerhouse Wish I Was A Headlight.
review: Both reviewers rave about the performance, pointing out standout moments like the China>Rider sequence, the final electric “Oh Babe, It Ain’t No Lie,” and a blistering Hell in a Bucket. The tape is praised for its clarity – every little sound can be heard – making it an enjoyable listen despite the unknown source type.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical praise with many standout songs and a clean, detailed recording; both reviews are 4‑star, indicating high overall quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Sound described as clear and detailed – “can hear every little sound.” No complaints about dropouts or muffling.

standout_songs: Oh Babe, It Ain't No Lie
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds nicely with strong vocal and instrumental work; the second set is noted for its unique amp issues, tight jams, and powerful transitions into Rider and the final electric ballad.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice in fine form, infectious Hey Now’s, delivered the final electric “Oh Babe, It Ain’t No Lie,” and added Spoonful later in the set.
band_member_comments.Phil: Opening of the show has Phil “all over the place,” suggesting a less focused but still present bass performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir delivers a very nice solo in the second jam, shines during transitions, and handles amp issues with style.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboardist) debuts his first version of “Don’t Need Love” and contributes throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Brief drum passages noted as “very short drums, cool space,” with standard fare later in the show.
